exemplar.title: Exploring the Convergence and Divergence of Different Infinite Series Using Various Convergence Tests

detailedExemplarData.title: Exploring the Convergence and Divergence of Different Infinite Series Using Various Convergence Tests

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear logical progression through introduction, body sections and conclusion
Well-structured with headings, sub-headings and table of contents
Mostly concise and relevant material with minimal digressions
weaknesses: Occasional minor lapses in logic when justifying test applicability
Missing page numbers and inconsistent TOC entries hinder navigation
Some redundant sentences and unnecessary standalone lines interrupt flow
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates a coherent and logically developed presentation (band 3). Headings and numbered sections give clear structure, and the content is mostly concise and relevant. However, minor issues (e.g. misplaced figures, incomplete citations, table of contents omissions) prevent an exceptional band 4. A score of 3 reflects strong but not flawless organization and coherence.

strengths: Mostly consistent and accurate use of mathematical language and notation
Step-by-step derivations with clear verbal explanations
Good logical structure enabling the reader to follow arguments
weaknesses: Limited variety of representationsâno self-generated tables or CAS outputs
Occasional notation slips (e.g. âar2â instead of exponent format)
Some omitted intermediate steps reduce absolute clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematical communication is clear and logically structured (band 3). Notation is generally accurate and verbal explanations support the derivations. The lack of self-generated representations and minor typographical errors keep it from band 4. Awarding band 3 captures its good clarity and mostly consistent notation.

strengths: Genuine personal connection to finance and perpetuity valuation
Clear personal voice in introduction and topic choice
weaknesses: Limited creativityâthe exploration follows standard convergence tests
Independent thinking is evident but not fully developed into innovative approach
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows clear evidence of personal engagement (band 2): a personal route through financial examples and justification of choices. Creativity and initiative, however, remain moderate, preventing a band 3. A score of 2 acknowledges significant but not outstanding personal engagement.

strengths: Meaningful reflections on choice and applicability of tests
Acknowledgment of limitations and learning outcomes
weaknesses: Surface-level critical analysis with limited probing of error sources
Brief evaluation of outcomes without detailed future implications
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is meaningful at times (band 2) with insights into test applicability and personal learning. Nonetheless, critical analysis and evaluation of outcomes are shallow, lacking depth and comprehensive future considerations. A band 2 score fairly represents this level of reflection.

strengths: Effective and varied selection of convergence tests (integral, ratio, limit comparison, alternating)
Mathematics fully commensurate with HL course level
Correct derivations with sound conceptual understanding
weaknesses: Minor omissions in justifying continuity and monotonicity conditions
Typographical and notation slips do not undermine overall validity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The use of mathematics is highly appropriate (between band 4 and band 5). A wide range of HL convergence techniques is applied correctly and interpreted accurately, demonstrating comprehensive mathematical use. Minor justification omissions and minor errors prevent an expert band 6, but warrant elevating to band 5 for its breadth and reliability.

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments are laid out step-by-step, with limits, substitutions and brief verbal explanations. The reader can follow the derivations without needing to fill major gaps. Occasional shorthand and typographical slips limit absolute clarity, but overall the logical flow meets the âgood logical structure and clear communicationâ level.

Derivations are largely correct; minor slips (typographical errors, informal wording) do not undermine the mathematical validity. The student shows good conceptual understanding of why each test works and interprets results accurately, consistent with âgood understanding and mostly correctâ. The work is not free of small errors, so band 5-6 is not justified.

The student supplies algebraic working, limits and two graphs illustrating the integral test. These satisfy âsome use of different formsâ. However, all visuals are borrowed, no self-generated tables or technology outputs are included, and the range is modest, so the work does not reach the âeffectiveâ criterion of band 3.

The exploration follows a clear progression: introduction, individual sections for each series with associated tests, and a conclusion. Each section builds logically on the previous one, allowing the reader to follow the overall argument without major confusion. Minor lapses in logic (e.g., brief jumps when justifying test applicability) prevent the work from reaching exemplary coherence, but the development is strong enough to merit the âcoherent and logically developedâ band.

The exploration applies well-known convergence tests exactly as found in standard references. Other than assembling them in one paper, little novelty or risk-taking is shown. This aligns with âsome creativity or initiativeâ (band 1) rather than the stronger descriptors.

The student notes that findings matched existing theory and mentions limitations, but offers only surface-level analysis of methods and results. There is little probing of error sources or robustness, placing the work at âbasic analysisâ (band 1).

Outcomes are stated correctly, yet evaluation is briefâfuture work is suggested in general terms without discussing wider mathematical or real-world implications. This fits the âlimited evaluation of outcomesâ descriptor.

Reflection is woven through the conclusion and evaluation, commenting on why certain tests did or did not apply and what was learned. The reflections go beyond mere description, giving âmeaningfulâ but not deeply critical insights, appropriate for band 2.

Mathematical notation is generally correct (Î£, limits, modulus, derivatives). Errors such as writing âar2â instead of âar^{2}â and occasional misuse of subscripts show slight inconsistency, but the language is mostly appropriate and symbols are used in an intelligible way throughout, fitting band 3.

A distinct personal voice appears in the introduction, justification of topic choice, and evaluation sections. The studentâs selection of series and tests indicates a clear personal route through the material, satisfying âclear evidence of a personal approachâ, but innovation remains moderate rather than âhighly personal and innovativeâ.

Most of the 18 pages are directly concerned with applying convergence tests. Repetition is limited and digressions are rare, although a few explanatory sentences on finance and several copied figures add some unnecessary length. Overall relevance is high and the write-up is fairly concise, matching the descriptor for band 3 rather than the extremely tight standard of band 4.

Integral, ratio, limitâcomparison, alternating-series, and comparison tests are all relevant and correctly selected for each series, demonstrating effective use of appropriate mathematics. The range is substantial but not yet comprehensive across HL calculus, aligning with band 4.

Choosing a collection of series, selecting which tests apply, and defending those choices shows the student worked beyond textbook examples. Although the mathematics is standard, linking it to personal interest in finance and questioning the harmonic series demonstrates significantâthough not outstandingâindependent thinking.

Headings, sub-headings, numbered sections and a table of contents give the piece a clear structure. The organisation enables the reader to locate material easily and see how different tests link to the series studied. Minor inconsistencies (missing page numbers in the table, occasional misplaced figures) show that the structure is not exceptional, but it is definitely âwell-organised with clear structureâ.

Infinite-series convergence tests are firmly within HL scope. The student applies them correctly and justifies eligibility conditions (positivity, monotonicity, etc.). This represents mathematics âfully commensurate with course levelâ, meriting band 4 rather than expert-level 5-6.

annotations.comment: Repeating the list of series tested adds redundancy; consolidate into one concise paragraph.
The title prominently identifies the subject and level of inquiry, providing clear context for the reader.
The inclusion of a standalone âPage Countâ line interrupts the academic flow and is unnecessary within the main body.
Consider adding a brief abstract or aim statement on the title page to outline objectives and scope.
The table of contents provides section names but omits page references, making navigation cumbersome.
The first TOC entry reads âIn traditional action,â which appears unrelated to the investigationâs contentâreview for accuracy.
Consider aligning all TOC entries in a consistent format and include page numbers for each section.
The introduction clearly defines infinite sequences and series, establishing foundational concepts effectively.
Footnotes interrupt readability; integrate key references in-text or use endnotes to enhance conciseness.
The personal connection to finance in perpetuity valuation demonstrates genuine engagement with real-world applications.
The final sentence trails off midâthought; complete the aim statement to clarify the scope of series under study.
The statement of limit comparison test conditions is conflatedâL=0 and L=â clauses need clearer separation.
Consider summarizing the four limit comparison outcomes in a small table to enhance multiple representations.
The limit calculation correctly shows that L=d, a constant >0, proving divergence via limit comparison.
The conclusion that integral test cannot apply due to an increasing derivative needs referencing derivative sign tests.
The generalised geometric seriesâ formula uses âar2â instead of proper exponent notation $$ar^2$$âadjust for consistency.
Use of both ratio and integral tests demonstrates effective selection of HL convergence techniques.
Including a small table of values of $$a_n$$ versus $$n$$ would improve clarity of how $$|r|$$ controls term size.
The graph visually supports divergence reasoning, but it is a stock image; generating your own CAS plot would be more authentic.
The definition of the generalised arithmetic series is accurately presented with summation notation.

The integral test application correctly computes $$
\int_1^\infty ar^x dx$$, but justification for continuity on $$[1,\infty)$$ is minimal.
Bibliography entries vary in style and are missing access dates; adopt a consistent citation convention.
The exploration shows clear personal interest in finance, linking theory to realâworld motivation effectively.
In the alternating series test section, rationale is sound but could be improved by referencing Leibnizâs original theorem source.
Reflection on why certain tests were inapplicable is noted but would benefit from deeper critical analysis.
The explanation of how the area under $$f(x)$$ bounds the series is clear and logically structured.
The harmonic series section repeats the definition style; consider referring back to the standard form to avoid redundancy.
The conditions for the integral test on $$f(x)=1/x$$ are systematically verified, demonstrating clear methodical communication.
In the limit calculation, the step âln 1=0â is omitted; include explicit evaluation to maintain rigor.
Describing how each test complements others in the evaluation section would deepen reflection on methodology.
The evaluation acknowledges limitations but lacks discussion on error sources or sensitivity of tests.
Suggest extending to p-series or root test as noted, and specify expected outcomes to sharpen future directions.
